title: cookie-session with secure: false — the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP and can be sniffed

vulnerable.detail: cookie-session is configured with secure: false, so the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can read the signed session and hijack the user's authenticated session. Set secure: true in production (behind `app.set('trust proxy', 1)` if terminating TLS at a proxy).

fixed.detail: cookie-session sets secure: true — the signed session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.

title: cors() with origin: true and credentials — reflects any caller's origin, leaking authenticated responses cross-site

vulnerable.detail: cors() is called with origin: true, which REFLECTS whatever Origin the request carries back in Access-Control-Allow-Origin. With credentials: true this defeats the browser's `*`-plus-credentials guard: every site the victim visits can make credentialed cross-origin calls and read the authenticated responses. Pin origin to an explicit allow-list (string, array, or a validating function), never `true`.

fixed.detail: cors() pins origin to an explicit trusted value (not the reflect-everything `true`) — cross-origin credentialed access is limited to that allow-list.

title: cors() configured with origin: "*" — any website can call the API with the user's session

vulnerable.detail: cors() is called with origin: "*", allowing ANY website to make cross-origin requests to this API. Combined with credentials, this lets a malicious page act as the logged-in user. Set origin to an explicit allow-list (a string, array, or function), not "*".

fixed.detail: cors() sets an explicit allow-list origin (not "*") — cross-origin access is restricted to trusted sites.
